title: Company Context
subtitle: What you do, who you serve, what's special
intro: We're building the AI's mental model of the company. Pretend you're explaining it to a smart new hire who's never heard of you. Auto-saves; come back anytime.

steps.title: The 30-second pitch
steps.intro: If someone asked at a wedding, what would you say?

fields.label: What does the company do?
fields.placeholder: Plain language. "We make small-batch X for Y kind of customer" is fine. The AI will get cleverer from here.

fields.label: Who do you serve?
fields.placeholder: The actual customer. Be specific — "women 25–45 who hike" beats "outdoor enthusiasts." If you serve multiple segments, list them and which is biggest.

fields.label: Your "why" — what would you say at the rotary club?
fields.placeholder: One or two sentences. Why does this company exist? What gets people up in the morning around here?

steps.title: The shape of the business
steps.intro: How the company actually makes money and where its energy goes.

fields.label: How big?
fields.placeholder: 12 employees, $4M revenue, 1 location in Louisville, etc. As rough or precise as you like.

fields.label: What pays the bills — your revenue streams
fields.placeholder:
List them, biggest to smallest. e.g.:
- DTC retail (60%) — Shopify
- Wholesale (30%) — 80 accounts
- Custom corporate gifts (10%) — referrals only

fields.label: What's growing — where you're putting energy
fields.placeholder: Subscription is the bet for the next two years / Wholesale into the Midwest / DTC retention is the focus this fall

steps.title: What's special — and what you're NOT
steps.intro: Both sides are useful. The "NOT" half is what keeps the AI from drifting generic.

fields.label: What's actually different about how you do it?
fields.placeholder: The thing competitors can't easily copy. Heritage / ingredients / process / philosophy / who's behind it / where it's made. Concrete beats abstract.

fields.label: What you are NOT
fields.placeholder: What kinds of things do you say no to? "Not a fast-fashion brand." "Not a tech company." "Not for the bargain shopper." The AI will respect these.

fields.label: Who else does what you do, and how are you different?
fields.placeholder:
Name 2–4 competitors and one line on how you differ. e.g.:
- Brand X — cheaper, mass market
- Brand Y — similar quality, no heritage story
- Brand Z — different category but we lose customers to them sometimes

steps.title: Recent wins & cautionary tales
steps.intro: Real anecdotes the AI can reference when drafting. The more specific, the more useful.

fields.label: A few recent wins / proud moments / case studies
fields.placeholder: What's worked in the last year or two? A campaign that landed, a customer story, a milestone. A few sentences each, not a polished press release.

fields.label: Things that have gone sideways — and what you learned
fields.placeholder: Optional but gold. A campaign that flopped, a product that didn't take, a customer-service moment you handled badly. Helps the AI not repeat the pattern.

steps.title: Guardrails
steps.intro: Topics, claims and tones the AI should stay away from.

fields.label: Topics the AI should NOT talk about for us
fields.placeholder: Politics. Specific health claims we're not allowed to make. Anything about the founder's personal life. Competitor names in customer-facing copy.

fields.label: Sensitive areas — handle with extra care
fields.placeholder: Returns policy (don't promise what isn't true). Wholesale pricing (varies by account). Anything about the supply chain (long story).

fields.label: Any other house rules?
fields.placeholder: We always credit suppliers by name. We never use "sustainable" without a footnote. We answer customer email within 24 hours.
